name: Basic Salsa with Any Kind of Dry Chiles
recipeIngredient: 6 large dry chiles (morita or mulato, or guajillo, or any kind)
If using smaller chiles like jalapeños or serranos, use 15 chiles.
recipeInstructions:
The type of chiles you use will determine the final flavor. You can experiment with different types or mix various chiles.
Wash the chiles in water and remove the seeds and stems.
Let stand in water for at least 2-3 hours, or overnight if possible. If time is limited, place them in warm water for at least 30 minutes.
Grind the chiles with the other ingredients.
In a saucepan, heat a small amount of vegetable oil until warm.
Add the salsa and cook for 30 minutes or more, allowing the flavor of vinegar to disappear.
If needed, add water to achieve desired consistency.
Use this salsa for dishes like "Chilaquiles," "Enchiladas," and eggs.
